mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month rise in industrial production signals expanding real output and corporate earnings capacity, which propagates into higher forward equity valuations as investors price in stronger profit growth over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=117.813 as of 2004-12-01 shows continued expansion phase
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive INDPRO trend periods show no statistically significant excess return versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 3.5% in a rising-rate cycle, higher discount rates on future cash flows and tightening financial conditions begin to outweigh earnings growth expectations, producing negative equity returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 3.5% while trending upward for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=2.28 as of 2005-01-01 in an active tightening cycle from 1.0% low
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS crossing 3.5% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect reverses sign across two consecutive tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth decelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously ticks up, the joint signal indicates an accelerating labor market deterioration that is not evident from either series alone, triggering rapid downward equity repricing within one week as markets reassess consumer spending capacity.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change turns negative or decelerates by >50% AND UNRATE rises by >=0.1pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.2 vintage vs 5.3 revised as of 2005-01-01 shows mild labor softening risk
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE uptick events produce negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or single-series UNRATE-only signal achieves equal or higher Rank IC than the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle housing recovery regime, a rising trend in housing starts reflects strengthening consumer demand and construction activity that feeds through into broader economic optimism, lifting equity prices over the subsequent month, but this relationship is muted or absent in late-cycle or contractionary regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=2042.0 thousand units as of 2004-12-01 indicates robust housing activity
failure_condition: HOUST uptrend fails to predict positive 20d forward returns with Rank IC below 0.03 during identified early-cycle periods, or the sign of the relationship flips to negative in more than one historical early-cycle episode.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in CPI month-over-month inflation above trend triggers fears of imminent monetary tightening, causing a short, sharp negative equity reaction within one trading week as markets reprice rate-hike probabilities.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=191.7 vs vintage 191.1 as of 2004-12-01 shows modest inflation drift
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following CPI upside surprises above threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, or effect size is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month uptrend in real retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, and predicts continued economic expansion and corporate revenue growth that supports equity prices over a full quarter horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value=321272.0 million as of 2005-01-01 reflecting steady consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month RSAFS growth trend and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or retail sales uptrend periods show no significant excess return over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Once the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, this threshold crossing signals a genuine labor market turning point rather than noise, and predicts negative equity returns over the following month as recession probability is repriced upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage=5.2 revised=5.3 as of 2005-01-01, near cycle lows with limited recent deterioration
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE threshold-crossing events are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the historical hit rate for this signal is not statistically different from the unconditional 65.5% positive base rate.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates while CPI inflation simultaneously accelerates, the joint stagflationary signal indicates margin compression risk for corporations that is not visible in either series independently, producing negative equity returns over a full quarter as earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth decelerates below 1% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds 3%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=117.813 vs revised_value=95.5365 shows large revision risk warranting joint confirmation
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO deceleration and CPI acceleration events produce negative 60d forward returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or the interaction signal underperforms either single-series signal in Rank IC over any 3-year window.
